optimizer_switches.name: batched_key_access
optimizer_switches.explanation: Collects keys from the outer table into the join buffer, sorts them, then uses MRR to fetch inner rows in (mostly) index order â€” cuts random I/O on large indexed joins.

optimizer_switches.name: join_cache_bka
optimizer_switches.explanation: MariaDB variant of batched_key_access: inner rows are fetched in batches keyed from the join buffer, using MRR to reorder I/O.

optimizer_switches.name: mrr
optimizer_switches.explanation: Multi-Range Read: sorts row IDs before fetching rows so InnoDB reads pages in roughly primary-key order, turning random I/O into sequential I/O on range scans.
node_labels: Multi-range index lookup on t1 using idx_a (a=t2.x)

suggestions.action: Add indexes on filter/join columns to avoid full table scans

executive_summary: Query scans 1 table; returns 24,950 rows in 19 ms. Main finding: no index covers (y, x) on t2.

query.raw: /* select#1 */ select straight_join `testdb`.`t1`.`id` AS `id`,`testdb`.`t1`.`a` AS `a`,`testdb`.`t1`.`b` AS `b`,`testdb`.`t1`.`c` AS `c`,`testdb`.`t1`.`d` AS `d` from `testdb`.`t2` join `testdb`.`t1` where ((`testdb`.`t1`.`a` = `testdb`.`t2`.`x`) and (`testdb`.`t2`.`y` < 1000))
query.beautified:
select straight_join testdb.t1.id AS id,testdb.t1.a AS a,testdb.t1.b AS b,testdb.t1.c AS c,testdb.t1.d AS d
from testdb.t2
  join testdb.t1
where ((testdb.t1.a = testdb.t2.x) and (testdb.t2.y < 1000))

index_suggestions.ddl: CREATE INDEX idx_t2_y_x ON t2 (y, x);
index_suggestions.reason: Full scan on t2 with filter on (y, x)

operator_complexities.folded_label: Batched key access inner join starts=1 rows=24950

complexity.rationale: Batched Key Access: outer keys are buffered and sorted, then the inner side is fetched via Multi-Range Read in clustered order. Cuts random I/O but keeps the index-descent cost per outer row.
Full table scan: the storage engine returns every row; cost scales with the table size.

operator_complexities.folded_label: Multi-range index lookup on t1 using idx_a (a=t2.x) starts=2 rows=12475
operator_complexities.short_label: Multi-range index lookup on t1 using idx_a (a=t2.x)

complexity.rationale: Index range scan: one B+tree descent to the range start, then a sequential walk over k matching index entries.
